channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: CR4T: Rewrite-Based Guardrails for Adolescent LLM Safety
item.description: arXiv:2605.21609v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly embedded in adolescent digital environments, mediating information seeking, advice, and emotionally sensitive interactions. Yet existing safety mechanisms remain largely grounded in adult-centric norms and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, LLM, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Broadening Access to Transportation Safety Data with Generative AI: A Schema-Grounded Framework for Spatial Natural Language Queries
item.description: arXiv:2605.21712v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transportation safety analysis requires integrating crash records, roadway attributes, and geospatial data through GIS-based workflows, but access remains uneven across agencies and community stakeholders. Technical prerequisites create a gap between ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, paper, LLM, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Sem-Detect: Semantic Level Detection of AI Generated Peer-Reviews
item.description: arXiv:2605.21713v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How can we distinguish whether a peer review was written by a human or generated by an AI model? We argue that, in this setting, authorship should not be attributed solely from the textual features of a review, but also from the ideas, judgments, and ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, paper, analysis, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Probabilistic Attribution For Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.21726v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The generative nature of Large Language Models (LLMs) is reflected in the conditional probabilities they compute to sample each response token given the previous tokens. These probabilities encode the distributional structure that the model learns in ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, context, arxiv, LLM, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PromptNCE: Pointwise Mutual Information Predictions Using Only LLMs and Contrastive Estimation Prompts
item.description: arXiv:2605.21776v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Estimating mutual information from text usually requires training a task-specific critic, which limits its use in low-data settings. We ask whether large language models can instead estimate pointwise mutual information zero-shot, using only prompts a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, LLM, study, prompting | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Reflective Prompt Tuning through Language Model Function-Calling
item.description: arXiv:2605.21781v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have become increasingly capable of following instructions and complex reasoning, making prompting a flexible interface for adapting models without parameter updates. Yet prompt design remains labor-intensive and highly se...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, LLM, instruction, prompting | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Residual Skill Optimization for Text-to-SQL Ensembles
item.description: arXiv:2605.21792v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Text-to-SQL ensembles improve over single-candidate generation by drawing multiple SQL candidates and selecting one, but their effectiveness is bounded by Pass@K, the probability that at least one of K candidates is correct. Existing methods source di...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, example, prompt, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When Cases Get Rare: A Retrieval Benchmark for Off-Guideline Clinical Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2605.21807v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Across medical specialties, clinical practice is anchored in evidence-based guidelines that codify best studied diagnostic and treatment pathways. These pathways routinely fall short for the long tail of real-world care not covered by guidelines. Most...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, context, arxiv, LLM, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Does Slightly Mean Somewhat? Measuring Vague Intensity Words in LLM Numeric Actions
item.description: arXiv:2605.21827v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Do language models preserve the ordinal meaning of intensity words when those words must produce numeric actions? I study a researcher-constructed scale of 10 English degree modifiers, from slightly to drastically, informed by the Quirk et al. degree-...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, context, LLM, study, instruction | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SOLAR: A Self-Optimizing Open-Ended Autonomous Agent for Lifelong Learning and Continual Adaptation
item.description: arXiv:2605.20189v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the remarkable success of large language models (LLMs), they still face bottlenecks while deploying in dynamic, real-world settings with primary challenges being concept drift and the high cost of gradient-based adaptation. Traditional fine-tu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, knowledge base, LLM, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Tool-Augmented Agent for Closed-loop Optimization,Simulation,and Modeling Orchestration
item.description: arXiv:2605.20190v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Iterative industrial design-simulation optimization is bottlenecked by the CAD-CAE semantic gap: translating simulation feedback into valid geometric edits under diverse, coupled constraints. To fill this gap, we propose COSMO-Agent (Closed-loop Optim...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, experiment, tool, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OSCToM: RL-Guided Adversarial Generation for High-Order Theory of Mind
item.description: arXiv:2605.20423v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) perform well on many language tasks, but their Theory of Mind (ToM) reasoning is still uneven in complex social settings. Existing benchmarks, including ExploreToM, do not always test the recursive beliefs and information ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, paper, LLM, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AgentCo-op: Retrieval-Based Synthesis of Interoperable Multi-Agent Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2605.20425v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Designing multi-agent workflows is especially difficult in open-ended scientific settings where tasks lack curated training sets, reliable scalar evaluation metrics, and standardized interfaces between existing tools and agents. We propose AgentCo-op,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, cross-modal, analysis, tool, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: High Quality Embeddings for Horn Logic Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2605.20467v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neural networks can be trained to rank the choices made by logical reasoners, resulting in more efficient searches for answers. A key step in this process is creating useful embeddings, i.e., numeric representations of logical statements. This paper i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, knowledge base, paper, experiment, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AgentAtlas: Beyond Outcome Leaderboards for LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.20530v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model agents now act on codebases, browsers, operating systems, calendars, files, and tool ecosystems, but the benchmarks used to evaluate them are fragmented: each emphasizes a different unit of measurement (final task success, tool-ca...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, context, arxiv, LLM, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mahjax: A GPU-Accelerated Mahjong Simulator for Reinforcement Learning in JAX
item.description: arXiv:2605.20577v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Riichi Mahjong is a multi-player, imperfect-information game characterized by stochasticity and high-dimensional state spaces. These attributes present a unique combination of challenges that mirror complex real-world decision-making problems in reinf...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, vector, experiment, research, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Temporal Contrastive Transformer for Financial Crime Detection: Self-Supervised Sequence Embeddings via Predictive Contrastive Coding
item.description: arXiv:2605.21490v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce the Temporal Contrastive Transformer (TCT), a representation learning framework designed to capture contextual temporal dynamics in sequences of financial transactions. The model is trained using a self-supervised contrastive objective to...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, context, product, experiment, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Teaching Language Models to Forecast Research Success Through Comparative Idea Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2605.21491v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As language models accelerate scientific research by automating hypothesis generation and implementation, a new bottleneck emerges: evaluating and filtering hundreds of AI-generated ideas without exhaustive experimentation. We ask whether LMs can lear...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, study, experiment, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HealthCraft: A Reinforcement Learning Safety Environment for Emergency Medicine
item.description: arXiv:2605.21496v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Frontier language models are being deployed into clinical workflows faster than the infrastructure to evaluate them safely. Static medical-QA benchmarks miss the failure modes that matter in emergency medicine: trajectory-level safety collapse, tool m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, release, LLM, API, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Harnesses for Inference-Time Alignment over Execution Trajectories
item.description: arXiv:2605.21516v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Harness engineering has emerged as an important inference-time technique for large language model (LLM) agents, aiming to improve long-term performance through task decomposition and guided execution. However, more elaborate harnesses are not uniforml...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, alignment, arxiv, LLM, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DualOptim+: Bridging Shared and Decoupled Optimizer States for Better Machine Unlearning in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.21539v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose DualOptim+, a novel optimization framework for improving machine unlearning in large language models. It introduces a base state to capture common representations shared by forgetting and retaining objectives and delta states to preserve ob...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, alignment, arxiv, experiment, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, prompt engineering, prompt, context window | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, RAG, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> knowledge base, LLM, vector, reasoning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.title: $ECUAS_n$: A family of metrics for principled evaluation of uncertainty-augmented systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.20490v2 Announce Type: new Abstract: In high-stakes automated decision-making, access to predictive uncertainty is essential for enabling users -- human or downstream systems -- to accept or reject predictions based on application-specific cost trade-offs. Such uncertainty-augmented (UA)...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, augmented, RAG, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Open-World Evaluations for Measuring Frontier AI Capabilities
item.description: arXiv:2605.20520v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Benchmark-based evaluation remains important for tracking frontier AI progress. But it can both overstate and understate deployed capability because it privileges tasks that can be precisely specified, automatically graded, easy to optimize for, and r...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, paper, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Attribution Impossibility: No Feature Ranking Is Faithful, Stable, and Complete Under Collinearity
item.description: arXiv:2605.21492v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: No feature ranking can be simultaneously faithful, stable, and complete when features are collinear. For collinear pairs, ranking reduces to a coin flip. We prove this impossibility, quantify it for four model classes, resolve it via ensemble averagin...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, tool, RAG, model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Predicting Performance of Symbolic and Prompt Programs with Examples
item.description: arXiv:2605.21515v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM prompting is widely used for naturally stated tasks, yet it is unreliable it may succeed on a few test cases but fail at deployment time. We study performance prediction: given a program, either symbolic (e.g. Python) or a prompt executed on an LL...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, study, prompting, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Reproducible Log-Driven AutoML Framework for Interpretable Pipeline Optimization in Healthcare Risk Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2605.21528v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate and reproducible disease risk prediction remains challenging due to heterogeneous features, limited samples, and severe class imbalance. This study introduces yvsoucom-iterkit, a deterministic and log-driven automated machine learning framewo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, experiment, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: RankJudge: A Multi-Turn LLM-as-a-Judge Synthetic Benchmark Generator
item.description: arXiv:2605.21748v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As interactive LLM-based applications are created and refined, model developers need to evaluate the quality of generated text along many possible axes. For simpler systems, human evaluation may be practical, but in complicated systems like conversati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Personality Engineering with AI Agents: A New Methodology for Negotiation Research
item.description: arXiv:2605.20554v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: According to canonical negotiation theory, people's success in a negotiation depends on how well they balance competing demands--empathizing and asserting, demonstrating concern for other and concern for self, being soft on the people and hard on the ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, ICL, experiment, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Don't Collapse Your Features: Why CenterLoss Hurts OOD Detection and Multi-Scale Mahalanobis Wins
item.description: arXiv:2605.21493v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The ability to detect out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs is fundamental to safe deployment of machine learning systems. Yet, current methods often rely on feature representations that are optimised solely for classification accuracy, neglecting the dist...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, RAG, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Double descent for least-squares interpolation on contaminated data: A simulation study
item.description: arXiv:2605.21494v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Overparametrized models can exhibit an excellent generalization performance, although they should be prone to overfitting according to classical statistical theory. The discovery of the "double descent", indicating that the generalization error decrea...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, model, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, example, step-by-step, prompt, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>

item.title: WrenAI - Turn any AI Agents into world-class data analysts through the open context layer that gives AI agents grounded, governed memory, context, SQL across 20+ data sources, that helps you build GenBI, agentic BI, text-to-sql, dashboards, and agentic analytics.
